title: Upload Admission, Untrusted Files, and Quarantine · Execution Trace: Upload Accessible Feedback
summary: An upload handler trusts client filenames and content types, accepts unlimited bodies, publishes bytes before scanning, and leaves partial state when the caller disconnects. Predict the representation, storage, trust, cost, and recovery evidence before inspecting a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the upload accessible feedback increment in bounded upload-admission and quarantine workflow. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and…
objectives: Expose preflight limits, progress, cancellation, validation details, recovery, and non-color status to keyboard and assistive-technology users.
Model admitted, uploading, quarantined, scanning, accepted, rejected, published, and deleted transitions with authorized owners.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict Upload Accessible Feedback · Theory Upload Accessible Feedback
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go file-delivery team handles case 6766 during a high-traffic evidence intake window. Build deterministic Go evidence to bound upload bytes and concurrency before quarantined publication state; cap bytes, time, attempts, concurrency, and spend before starting work; then change the object version and prove stale authority is rejected. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Expose preflight limits, progress, cancellation, validation details, recovery, and non-color status to keyboard and assistive-technology users.
